tEn: Draft, Sketch, and Prove: Guiding Formal Theorem Provers with Informal Proofs
tZh: 草稿、草图与证明：用非形式化证明指导形式化定理证明器
sEn: Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) uses informal proofs to guide formal theorem provers, improving success rate from 20.9% to 39.3% on math competition problems.
sZh: DSP方法利用非形式化证明指导形式化定理证明器，在数学竞赛问题集上将成功率从20.9%提升至39.3%。
absEn: The formalization of existing mathematical proofs is a notoriously difficult process. Despite decades of research on automation and proof assistants, writing formal proofs remains arduous and only accessible to a few experts. While previous studies to automate formalization focused on powerful search algorithms, no attempts were made to take advantage of available informal proofs. In this work, we introduce Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP), a method that maps informal proofs to formal proof sketches, and uses the sketches to guide an automated prover by directing its search to easier sub-problems. We investigate two relevant setups where informal proofs are either written by humans or generated by a language model. Our experiments and ablation studies show that large language models are able to produce well-structured formal sketches that follow the same reasoning steps as the informal proofs. Guiding an automated prover with these sketches enhances its performance from 20.9% to 39.3% on a collection of mathematical competition problems.
absZh: 现有数学证明的形式化是一个众所周知的困难过程。尽管在自动化和证明助手方面进行了数十年的研究，编写形式化证明仍然艰巨且只有少数专家能够掌握。以往自动化形式化的研究侧重于强大的搜索算法，但没有人尝试利用现有的非形式化证明。在这项工作中，我们引入了Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP)方法，该方法将非形式化证明映射为形式化证明草图，并利用这些草图通过将搜索引导到更简单的子问题来指导自动化证明器。我们研究了两种相关设置，其中非形式化证明由人类编写或由语言模型生成。我们的实验和消融研究表明，大型语言模型能够生成结构良好的形式化草图，这些草图遵循与非形式化证明相同的推理步骤。用这些草图指导自动化证明器，在一组数学竞赛问题上的性能从20.9%提升到了39.3%。

contrib.en: Introduces Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP), a method that maps informal proofs to formal proof sketches to guide automated provers.
contrib.zh: 提出 Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) 方法，将非形式证明映射为形式证明草图以引导自动证明器。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that large language models can generate well-structured formal sketches following informal reasoning steps.
contrib.zh: 证明大型语言模型能生成遵循非形式推理步骤的结构良好的形式草图。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art performance on miniF2F with Isabelle, increasing success rate from 20.9% to 39.3% using human-written proofs.
contrib.zh: 在 miniF2F 上使用 Isabelle 达到最先进性能，使用人类编写的证明将成功率从 20.9% 提升至 39.3%。

contrib.en: Shows that language-model-generated informal proofs are nearly as effective as human-written ones in guiding formal provers.
contrib.zh: 表明语言模型生成的非形式证明在引导形式证明器方面几乎与人类编写的同样有效。

contrib.en: Provides ablation studies confirming the importance of drafting informal proofs, annotating sketches with comments, and using automated provers.
contrib.zh: 通过消融研究确认了草拟非形式证明、用注释标注草图以及使用自动证明器的重要性。

contrib.en: Releases a curated dataset of informal statements and proofs aligned with miniF2F formal statements.
contrib.zh: 发布与 miniF2F 形式陈述对齐的非形式陈述和证明的精选数据集。

limits.en: Method is evaluated only on high-school competition problems in miniF2F, limiting generalizability to advanced mathematics.
limits.zh: 方法仅在 miniF2F 的高中竞赛问题上评估，限制了向高等数学的泛化能力。

limits.en: Relies on large language models (e.g., Codex, Minerva) which may not be accessible or reproducible due to API costs.
limits.zh: 依赖大型语言模型（如 Codex、Minerva），由于 API 成本可能无法访问或复现。

limits.en: Performance is bounded by the automated prover's ability to fill gaps; complex conjectures may remain unprovable.
limits.zh: 性能受限于自动证明器填补空白的能力；复杂猜想可能仍无法证明。

limits.en: Informal proofs from language models can be incorrect, yet still lead to successful formal proofs, indicating potential unreliability.
limits.zh: 语言模型生成的非形式证明可能不正确，但仍能产生成功的形式证明，表明潜在不可靠性。

limits.en: The method requires manual preparation of few-shot examples for autoformalization, which may not scale to diverse domains.
limits.zh: 该方法需要手动准备少量示例用于自动形式化，可能无法扩展到多样化领域。

items.en: The formalization of existing mathematical proofs is a notoriously difficult process. Despite decades of research on automation and proof assistants, writing formal proofs remains arduous and only accessible to a few experts. While previous studies to automate formalization focused on powerful search algorithms, no attempts were made to take advantage of available informal proofs. In this work, we introduce Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP), a method that maps informal proofs to formal proof sketches, and uses the sketches to guide an automated prover by directing its search to easier sub-problems. We investigate two relevant setups where informal proofs are either written by humans or generated by a language model. Our experiments and ablation studies show that large language models are able to produce well-structured formal sketches that follow the same reasoning steps as the informal proofs. Guiding an automated prover with these sketches enhances its performance from 20.9%percent20.920.9\% to 39.3%percent39.339.3\% on a collection of mathematical competition problems.
items.zh: 现有数学证明的形式化是一个众所周知的困难过程。尽管在自动化和证明助手方面进行了数十年的研究，编写形式化证明仍然很艰巨，只有少数专家能够做到。虽然先前关于自动化形式化的研究侧重于强大的搜索算法，但没有人尝试利用现有的非形式化证明。在这项工作中，我们引入了 Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) 方法，该方法将非形式化证明映射到形式化证明草图，并使用这些草图通过将搜索引导到更简单的子问题来指导自动化证明器。我们研究了两种相关的设置，其中非形式化证明要么由人类编写，要么由语言模型生成。我们的实验和消融研究表明，大型语言模型能够生成结构良好的形式化草图，这些草图遵循与非形式化证明相同的推理步骤。使用这些草图指导自动化证明器，将其在数学竞赛问题集合上的性能从 20.9% 提高到 39.3%。

items.capEn: Figure 1: Draft, Sketch, and Prove. Starting with an informal statement, our framework yields a formal proof through a three-stage process: drafting informal proofs, mapping them into formal sketches, and proving the remaining conjectures. Concretely, an informal statement is a mathematical problem described in a mixture of natural and mathematical languages (e.g., formulae in LaTeX). Then, we use a large language model to autoformalize each informal proof into a formal sketch, which is a skeleton of the formal proof with open conjectures left unproven (indicated by the <proof> blocks). The formal sketch mirrors the structure of the informal proof. Finally, the open conjectures/gaps inside each formal sketch are proved by an off-the-shelf prover.
items.capZh: 图 1：Draft, Sketch, and Prove。从一个非形式化陈述开始，我们的框架通过三个阶段生成形式化证明：起草非形式化证明，将其映射到形式化草图，并证明剩余的猜想。具体来说，非形式化陈述是一个用自然语言和数学语言（例如 LaTeX 公式）混合描述的数学问题。然后，我们使用大型语言模型将每个非形式化证明自动形式化为形式化草图，该草图是形式化证明的骨架，其中包含未证明的开放猜想（由 <proof> 块指示）。形式化草图反映了非形式化证明的结构。最后，每个形式化草图内的开放猜想/间隙由现成的证明器证明。

items.en: Formal proof automation is a challenging task that has been the focus of increased attention in recent years (Bansal et al., 2019b; Polu & Sutskever, 2020; Lample et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2022; Wu et al., 2022). However, deep learning approaches have not been as successful as in other domains, mainly because of the scarcity of formal data. Indeed, formalizing proofs is notoriously difficult and only accessible to a handful of experts, which makes large annotation endeavors unrealistic (Wiedijk, 2008). The largest formal proof corpus is written in Isabelle (Paulson, 1994), and amounts to less than 0.60.60.6 GB in size, orders of magnitude smaller than datasets commonly used in vision (Deng et al., 2009) or natural language processing (Brown et al., 2020). To address the scarcity of formal proofs, previous studies have proposed to use synthetic data (Wu et al., 2021), self-supervision (Polu & Sutskever, 2020; Han et al., 2022), or reinforcement learning (Bansal et al., 2019a; Polu et al., 2022) to synthesize additional formal training data. Although these methods alleviate the data insufficiency to some degree, none are able to capitalize on the bulk of human-written mathematical proofs.
items.zh: 形式化证明自动化是一项具有挑战性的任务，近年来受到越来越多的关注（Bansal 等人，2019b；Polu & Sutskever，2020；Lample 等人，2022；Jiang 等人，2022；Wu 等人，2022）。然而，深度学习方法在其他领域并未取得同样成功，主要是因为形式化数据的稀缺性。事实上，形式化证明是出了名的困难，只有少数专家才能做到，这使得大规模的标注工作不切实际（Wiedijk，2008）。最大的形式化证明语料库是用 Isabelle（Paulson，1994）编写的，大小不到 0.6 GB，比视觉（Deng 等人，2009）或自然语言处理（Brown 等人，2020）中常用的数据集小几个数量级。为了解决形式化证明的稀缺性，先前的研究提出了使用合成数据（Wu 等人，2021）、自监督（Polu & Sutskever，2020；Han 等人，2022）或强化学习（Bansal 等人，2019a；Polu 等人，2022）来合成额外的形式化训练数据。尽管这些方法在一定程度上缓解了数据不足的问题，但没有一种方法能够利用人类编写的大量数学证明。

items.en: Unlike formal mathematics, informal mathematical data is abundant and widely available. Recently, large language models trained on informal mathematical data showcased impressive quantitative reasoning abilities (Lewkowycz et al., 2022; Welleck et al., 2022). However, they often generate erroneous proofs and it is challenging to detect the faulty reasoning in these proofs automatically. Our work devises a novel approach called Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) to translate informal mathematical proofs into formal ones and thus enjoy both the logical rigor provided by formal systems and the wealth of informal data. We give a schematic diagram of the DSP method in Figure 1 and describe it in Section 3. Recent work (Wu et al., 2022) demonstrates the feasibility of automatically translating informal statements into formal ones with large language models. DSP goes beyond and leverages large language models to generate formal proof sketches (Wiedijk, 2003) from informal proofs. Proof sketches consist of high-level reasoning steps that can be interpreted by formal systems such as interactive theorem provers. They differ from complete formal proofs in that they contain sequences of intermediate conjectures without justification. An example of informal proof with its corresponding formal proof sketch is provided in Figure 2. In the last step of DSP, we elaborate the formal proof sketch into a full formal proof using an automated prover to prove all intermediate conjectures.
items.zh: 与非形式化数学不同，非形式化数学数据丰富且广泛可用。最近，在非形式化数学数据上训练的大型语言模型展示了令人印象深刻的定量推理能力（Lewkowycz 等人，2022；Welleck 等人，2022）。然而，它们经常生成错误的证明，并且很难自动检测这些证明中的错误推理。我们的工作设计了一种称为 Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) 的新方法，将非形式化数学证明翻译成形式化证明，从而同时享受形式化系统提供的逻辑严谨性和非形式化数据的丰富性。我们在图 1 中给出了 DSP 方法的示意图，并在第 3 节中进行了描述。最近的工作（Wu 等人，2022）证明了使用大型语言模型自动将非形式化陈述翻译成形式化陈述的可行性。DSP 更进一步，利用大型语言模型从非形式化证明生成形式化证明草图（Wiedijk，2003）。证明草图由高级推理步骤组成，可以被交互式定理证明器等形式化系统解释。它们与完整的形式化证明不同，因为它们包含一系列中间猜想而没有证明。图 2 提供了一个非形式化证明及其对应的形式化证明草图的示例。在 DSP 的最后一步，我们使用自动化证明器将形式化证明草图扩展为完整的形式化证明，以证明所有中间猜想。

items.en: We perform experiments to generate formal proofs of problems from the miniF2F dataset (Zheng et al., 2022) and show that a large portion of theorems can be proved automatically with this method. We investigate two settings where the informal proofs are either written by humans or drafted by a large language model trained on mathematical text. These two settings correspond to situations frequently occurring during the formalization of existing theories, where informal proofs are usually available, but sometimes left as exercises to the reader or missing due to space limits in the margin.
items.zh: 我们进行了实验，从 miniF2F 数据集（Zheng 等人，2022）生成问题的形式化证明，并表明通过这种方法可以自动证明大部分定理。我们研究了两种设置，其中非形式化证明要么由人类编写，要么由在数学文本上训练的大型语言模型起草。这两种设置对应于现有理论形式化过程中经常出现的情况，其中非形式化证明通常可用，但有时作为练习留给读者，或者由于页边空白空间限制而缺失。

items.en: We introduce a novel approach to leverage informal proofs to guide automated provers with formal proof sketches.
items.zh: 我们引入了一种新方法，利用非形式化证明通过形式化证明草图来指导自动化证明器。

items.en: To evaluate our approach, we build a dataset of manually curated informal statements and informal proofs aligned with formal statements in the miniF2F dataset (Zheng et al., 2022).
items.zh: 为了评估我们的方法，我们构建了一个数据集，其中包含与 miniF2F 数据集（Zheng 等人，2022）中的形式化陈述对齐的人工策划的非形式化陈述和非形式化证明。

items.en: We increase the proportion of problems solved by an automated prover on miniF2F from 20.9%percent20.920.9\% to 38.9%percent38.938.9\% given language-model-generated informal proofs, and up to 39.3%percent39.339.3\% when proofs are written by humans.
items.zh: 我们将自动化证明器在 miniF2F 上解决的问题比例从 20.9% 提高到 38.9%（使用语言模型生成的非形式化证明），以及提高到 39.3%（当证明由人类编写时）。

items.en: Through three ablation studies, we demonstrate the performance benefit of drafting informal proofs, annotating sketches with informal segments, and using automated provers to close open conjectures for the autoformalization of proofs.
items.zh: 通过三项消融研究，我们证明了起草非形式化证明、用非形式化片段注释草图以及使用自动化证明器关闭开放猜想对证明自动形式化的性能提升。

items.en: Modern verification systems for mathematics are centered around interactive theorem provers (ITPs), such as Isabelle (Paulson, 1994), Lean (Moura et al., 2015), Coq (Barras et al., 1997), or Metamath (Megill & Wheeler, 2019). ITPs embed the mathematical definitions and theorems onto a solid logical foundation (e.g., Higher-Order Logic, Dependent Type Theory) implemented by their kernels. Every theorem must be checked by the kernel to be recognized by the ITP. To be proved formally, a theorem is first stated in the ITP’s programming language, and iteratively simplified into simpler objectives (or subgoals), until it can be reduced to already proven facts. In this paper, we will refer to proofs verified by a formal theorem prover as formal proofs, and proofs written in “standard” mathematics (e.g. in LaTeX) as informal proofs.
items.zh: 现代数学验证系统以交互式定理证明器（ITP）为中心，例如 Isabelle（Paulson，1994）、Lean（Moura 等人，2015）、Coq（Barras 等人，1997）或 Metamath（Megill & Wheeler，2019）。ITP 将数学定义和定理嵌入到由内核实现的坚实逻辑基础（例如高阶逻辑、依赖类型理论）上。每个定理必须由内核检查才能被 ITP 识别。为了被形式化证明，定理首先用 ITP 的编程语言陈述，并迭代地简化为更简单的目标（或子目标），直到可以归结为已证明的事实。在本文中，我们将由形式化定理证明器验证的证明称为形式化证明，将“标准”数学（例如 LaTeX）中编写的证明称为非形式化证明。

items.en: Several approaches propose to combine machine learning with modern interactive theorem provers (Yang & Deng, 2019; Gauthier et al., 2021), and build upon the recent success of language models (Polu & Sutskever, 2020; Han et al., 2022; Polu et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2022; Lample et al., 2022). These methods typically rely on sequence-to-sequence models (Sutskever et al., 2014) to generate the next step of a proof given the current proof state and perform search over the generated subgoals using powerful search methods such as MCTS (Silver et al., 2018). Because search is computationally expensive, these language models are relatively small (with fewer than 111 billion parameters). Our method contrasts with these approaches in that we use a significantly reduced number of calls to the models, but also much larger language models (with up to 175175175 billion parameters) that showcase outstanding few-shot learning abilities (Brown et al., 2020).
items.zh: 有几种方法提出将机器学习与现代交互式定理证明器相结合（Yang & Deng，2019；Gauthier 等人，2021），并建立在语言模型最近的成功之上（Polu & Sutskever，2020；Han 等人，2022；Polu 等人，2022；Jiang 等人，2022；Lample 等人，2022）。这些方法通常依赖于序列到序列模型（Sutskever 等人，2014）根据当前证明状态生成证明的下一步，并使用强大的搜索方法（如 MCTS（Silver 等人，2018））对生成的子目标进行搜索。由于搜索计算成本高，这些语言模型相对较小（参数少于 10 亿）。我们的方法与这些方法不同，我们显著减少了模型的调用次数，但使用了更大的语言模型（参数高达 1750 亿），这些模型展示了出色的少样本学习能力（Brown 等人，2020）。

items.en: Language models have also been used in the context of purely informal mathematics (Lample & Charton, 2020; Hendrycks et al., 2021; Welleck et al., 2021; Drori et al., 2022; Welleck et al., 2022). Nevertheless, Lewkowycz et al. (2022) note that for quantitative question answering, models are prone to generate false positives: the model guesses the right answer while providing an incorrect proof. These errors are hard to spot without human inspection. Worryingly, the frequency of false positives increases with the difficulty of the problem. Our method builds on these findings and translates informal proofs into formal proofs. Since ITPs are logically grounded, once a formal proof is checked by them, we are guaranteed its correctness.
items.zh: 语言模型也被用于纯粹的非形式化数学中（Lample & Charton，2020；Hendrycks 等人，2021；Welleck 等人，2021；Drori 等人，2022；Welleck 等人，2022）。然而，Lewkowycz 等人（2022）指出，对于定量问答，模型容易产生假阳性：模型猜对了答案，但提供了不正确的证明。这些错误在没有人工检查的情况下很难发现。令人担忧的是，假阳性的频率随着问题难度的增加而增加。我们的方法基于这些发现，将非形式化证明翻译成形式化证明。由于 ITP 在逻辑上是严谨的，一旦形式化证明被它们检查，我们就保证了其正确性。

items.en: In a position paper, Szegedy (2020) argued for attaining formal mathematical data from informal sources with neural networks. Wang et al. (2020) performed preliminary experiments where the evaluation was limited to text-level similarities on synthetic datasets. Recently, Wu et al. (2022) found that large language models (Chen et al., 2021; Chowdhery et al., 2022) are capable of few-shot statement autoformalization. Namely, a small number of examples are enough for them to learn to perform informal-to-formal translation of statements. In this paper, we investigate whether these findings can generalize to proof autoformalization, i.e., whether large language models can be used to translate informal proofs into formal ones.
items.zh: 在一篇立场论文中，Szegedy（2020）主张通过神经网络从非形式化来源获取形式化数学数据。Wang 等人（2020）进行了初步实验，评估仅限于合成数据集上的文本级相似性。最近，Wu 等人（2022）发现大型语言模型（Chen 等人，2021；Chowdhery 等人，2022）能够进行少样本陈述自动形式化。也就是说，少量示例就足以让它们学习执行陈述的非形式化到形式化翻译。在本文中，我们研究这些发现是否可以推广到证明自动形式化，即大型语言模型是否可用于将非形式化证明翻译成形式化证明。

items.en: In this section, we describe our Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) method for formal proof automation, which leverages informal proofs to guide automated formal theorem provers with proof sketches. We assume that each problem comes with an informal statement and a formal statement describing the problem. Our pipeline consists of three stages (depicted in Figure 1), which we present below.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们描述了我们的 Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP) 方法，用于形式化证明自动化，该方法利用非形式化证明通过证明草图指导自动化形式化定理证明器。我们假设每个问题都附带一个非形式化陈述和一个描述问题的形式化陈述。我们的流程包括三个阶段（如图 1 所示），下面我们将介绍这些阶段。

items.en: The initial phase of the DSP method consists in finding informal proofs for a problem according to its description in natural mathematical language (possibly with LaTeX). The resulting informal proof is seen as a draft for the subsequent phases. In mathematical textbooks, proofs of theorems are in general provided, but are sometimes missing or incomplete. Therefore, we consider two settings corresponding to the presence or absence of the informal proofs. In the first, we assume that a “ground-truth” informal proof (i.e., one written by a human) is available, which is the typical scenario in the practice of formalizing existing mathematical theories. In the second setting, we make a more general assumption that the ground-truth informal proof is not given, and draft proof candidates with a large language model trained on informal mathematical data. The language model removes the dependence on human proofs and can produce multiple alternative solutions for every problem. Although there is no easy way to automatically verify the correctness of these proofs, the informal proof only needs to be useful for producing a good formal proof sketch in the next stage.
items.zh: DSP 方法的第一阶段是根据自然数学语言（可能带有 LaTeX）中的问题描述找到非形式化证明。得到的非形式化证明被视为后续阶段的草稿。在数学教科书中，定理的证明通常提供，但有时缺失或不完整。因此，我们考虑两种设置，对应于非形式化证明的存在或缺失。在第一种设置中，我们假设存在一个“真实”的非形式化证明（即由人类编写的），这是现有数学理论形式化实践中的典型情况。在第二种设置中，我们做出更一般的假设，即没有给出真实的非形式化证明，并使用在非形式化数学数据上训练的大型语言模型起草证明候选。语言模型消除了对人类证明的依赖，并且可以为每个问题生成多个替代解决方案。尽管没有简单的方法自动验证这些证明的正确性，但非形式化证明只需要在下一阶段对生成良好的形式化证明草图有用。

full.sec: 3.2 Mapping informal proofs into formal sketches
full.secZh: 3.2 将非形式化证明映射为形式化草图

items.en: A formal proof sketch encodes the structure of a solution and leaves out low-level details (Wiedijk, 2003). Intuitively, it is a partial proof that outlines high-level conjecture statements. A concrete example of a proof sketch is shown in Figure 2. Although informal proofs often leave aside low-level details, (e.g., by stating their triviality), these details cannot be discharged in a formal proof, making straightforward informal-to-formal proof translation difficult. Instead, we propose to map informal proofs to formal proof sketches that share the same high-level structures. The low-level details missing from a proof sketch can later be filled by an automated prover. Since large informal-formal parallel corpora do not exist, standard machine translation methods are unsuitable for this task. Rather, we use the few-shot learning abilities of a large language model. Specifically, we prompt the model with a few example pairs containing informal proofs and their corresponding formal sketches, followed by an informal proof yet to be translated. We then let the model generate the subsequent tokens to obtain the desired formal sketch. We refer to this model as an autoformalizer.
items.zh: 形式化证明草图编码了解的结构，并省略了低级细节（Wiedijk，2003）。直观地说，它是一个部分证明，概述了高级猜想陈述。图 2 显示了一个证明草图的具体示例。尽管非形式化证明通常省略低级细节（例如，通过说明其平凡性），但这些细节不能在形式化证明中省略，这使得直接的非形式化到形式化证明翻译变得困难。相反，我们建议将非形式化证明映射到具有相同高级结构的形式化证明草图。证明草图中缺失的低级细节稍后可以由自动化证明器填充。由于大型的非形式化-形式化平行语料库不存在，标准的机器翻译方法不适合此任务。相反，我们利用大型语言模型的少样本学习能力。具体来说，我们用几个示例对提示模型，其中包含非形式化证明及其对应的形式化草图，然后是一个尚未翻译的非形式化证明。然后，我们让模型生成后续标记以获得所需的形式化草图。我们将此模型称为自动形式化器。

full.sec: 3.3 Proving open conjectures in the sketches
full.secZh: 3.3 证明草图中的开放猜想

items.en: As the last part of the process, we execute off-the-shelf automated provers to fill in the missing details in proof sketches, where “automated provers” refers to systems capable of producing formally verifiable proofs. Our framework is agnostic to the specific choice of the automated prover: it can be symbolic provers such as heuristic proof automation tools, neural-network-based provers, or hybrid approaches. If the automated prover successfully closes all the gaps in the proof sketch, it returns the final formal proof which can be checked against the problem’s specification. If the automated prover fails (e.g., it exceeds the allocated time limit), we consider the evaluation to be unsuccessful.
items.zh: 作为过程的最后一部分，我们执行现成的自动化证明器来填充证明草图中缺失的细节，其中“自动化证明器”指能够生成可形式化验证的证明的系统。我们的框架与自动化证明器的具体选择无关：它可以是符号证明器（如启发式证明自动化工具）、基于神经网络的证明器或混合方法。如果自动化证明器成功关闭了证明草图中的所有间隙，它将返回最终的形式化证明，该证明可以对照问题的规范进行检查。如果自动化证明器失败（例如，超过分配的时间限制），我们认为评估不成功。

items.en: We evaluate our method on the miniF2F dataset (Zheng et al., 2022). The dataset contains the formal statements of 488488488 problems from high-school mathematical competitions, written in three formal languages: Lean, HOL-Light, and Isabelle. They are split into a valid set and a test set, composed of 244244244 problems each. In this work, we choose to experiment with Isabelle for three reasons: (1) Isabelle’s proof corpus is one of the largest among interactive theorem provers, conducive to the language models’ mastery of its syntax; (2) Isabelle supports the declarative proof style (detailed discussion in Appendix A), enabling formal proof sketches (Wiedijk, 2003) which are central to our method; (3) although automated proving tools are available in other interactive theorem provers, none are as developed and effective as Sledgehammer (Paulson, 2010) in Isabelle for proving conjectures.
items.zh: 我们在 miniF2F 数据集（Zheng 等人，2022）上评估我们的方法。该数据集包含来自高中数学竞赛的 488 个问题的形式化陈述，用三种形式化语言编写：Lean、HOL-Light 和 Isabelle。它们分为验证集和测试集，各包含 244 个问题。在这项工作中，我们选择使用 Isabelle 进行实验，原因有三：（1）Isabelle 的证明语料库是交互式定理证明器中最大的之一，有利于语言模型掌握其语法；（2）Isabelle 支持声明式证明风格（详细讨论见附录 A），使得形式化证明草图（Wiedijk，2003）成为可能，而这是我们的方法的核心；（3）尽管其他交互式定理证明器中也有自动化证明工具，但没有一个像 Isabelle 中的 Sledgehammer（Paulson，2010）那样发达和有效，用于证明猜想。

items.en: The miniF2F dataset is comprised of problems from three source categories: (1) 260260260 problems sampled from the MATH dataset (Hendrycks et al., 2021); (2) 160160160 problems from actual high-school mathematical competitions (AMC, AIME, and IMO); (3) 686868 crafted problems at the same difficulty level as (2). We employ three methods to obtain informal statements and proofs from these sources. For source (1), we access the informal statements and proofs from the MATH dataset; for (2), we retrieve their informal statements and proofs from the AOPS website 111https://artofproblemsolving.com/community; and for (3), we manually write down their informal statements and proofs. Thus we gather a parallel set of 488488488 informal statements, informal proofs, and formal statements. This dataset provides the informal statements and proofs for our experiment in the human-as-informal-proof-writer setting and is available at github.com/facebookresearch/miniF2F. This repository also contains fixes to numerous errors in the miniF2F dataset. In our experiments, we used formal statements from the original miniF2F repository 222https://github.com/openai/miniF2F for a fair comparison with prior works.
items.zh: miniF2F 数据集包含来自三个来源类别的问题：（1）从 MATH 数据集（Hendrycks 等人，2021）中采样的 260 个问题；（2）来自实际高中数学竞赛（AMC、AIME 和 IMO）的 160 个问题；（3）与（2）难度相同的 68 个精心设计的问题。我们采用三种方法从这些来源获取非形式化陈述和证明。对于来源（1），我们从 MATH 数据集获取非形式化陈述和证明；对于（2），我们从 AOPS 网站 1 检索其非形式化陈述和证明；对于（3），我们手动编写其非形式化陈述和证明。因此，我们收集了一个包含 488 个非形式化陈述、非形式化证明和形式化陈述的平行集。该数据集为我们在人类作为非形式化证明编写者设置下的实验提供了非形式化陈述和证明，并可在 github.com/facebookresearch/miniF2F 获取。该存储库还包含对 miniF2F 数据集中众多错误的修复。在我们的实验中，我们使用了原始 miniF2F 存储库 2 中的形式化陈述，以便与先前工作进行公平比较。

items.en: Our task is to generate formal proofs for problems as they are formally stated in miniF2F. We consider a proof valid if and only if it (a) does not contain “cheating” keywords (sorry and oops) that exit a proof without completing it, and (b) Isabelle is able to verify the corresponding formal statement with the proof. We use the Portal-to-ISAbelle API by Jiang et al. (2021) to interact with Isabelle.
items.zh: 我们的任务是为 miniF2F 中形式化陈述的问题生成形式化证明。我们认为一个证明有效当且仅当它（a）不包含“作弊”关键词（sorry 和 oops），这些关键词会在未完成证明的情况下退出证明，并且（b）Isabelle 能够使用该证明验证相应的形式化陈述。我们使用 Jiang 等人（2021）的 Portal-to-ISAbelle API 与 Isabelle 交互。

items.en: As a baseline, we attempt to prove the formal statement directly with Sledgehammer, a popular proof automation tool in Isabelle. We use the default Sledgehammer configuration in Isabelle2021, including a 120-second timeout and the five automated theorem provers (Z3, CVC4, SPASS, Vampire, E). Appendix B gives a more thorough introduction to Sledgehammer.
items.zh: 作为基线，我们尝试直接用Sledgehammer（Isabelle中一种流行的证明自动化工具）证明形式化语句。我们使用Isabelle2021中的默认Sledgehammer配置，包括120秒超时和五个自动定理证明器（Z3、CVC4、SPASS、Vampire、E）。附录B对Sledgehammer进行了更详细的介绍。

items.en: Occasionally, Sledgehammer may fail without trying simple yet effective tactics. As a second, stronger baseline, we create an automated prover that tries 111111 common tactics (auto, simp, blast, fastforce, force, eval, presburger, sos, arith, linarith, auto simp: field_simps) for high-school level algebra and number theory problems. If every attempted tactic fails, or times out after 101010 seconds, it falls back to Sledgehammer.
items.zh: 有时，Sledgehammer可能在不尝试简单但有效的策略的情况下失败。作为第二个更强的基线，我们创建了一个自动证明器，它尝试111111种常见策略（auto, simp, blast, fastforce, force, eval, presburger, sos, arith, linarith, auto simp: field_simps），用于高中水平的代数和数论问题。如果所有尝试的策略都失败，或在101010秒后超时，则回退到Sledgehammer。

items.en: Finally, we include baselines which are representative of state-of-the-art neural theorem proving in Isabelle, specifically Thor (Jiang et al., 2022) and Thor with expert iteration on autoformalized data (Wu et al., 2022). The methods GPT-f with expert iteration (Polu et al., 2022), and HyperTree Proof Search (HTPS) (Lample et al., 2022) can solve 36.6%percent36.636.6\% and 41.0%percent41.041.0\% of the problems on miniF2F-test. However, they rely on the Lean theorem prover instead of Isabelle, which greatly influences the performance due to the different tactics and automation, and are not directly comparable to our method.
items.zh: 最后，我们包含了代表Isabelle中神经定理证明最先进水平的基线，特别是Thor（Jiang等人，2022）和在自动形式化数据上使用专家迭代的Thor（Wu等人，2022）。使用专家迭代的GPT-f方法（Polu等人，2022）和HyperTree Proof Search（HTPS）（Lample等人，2022）可以在miniF2F测试集上解决36.6%和41.0%的问题。然而，它们依赖于Lean定理证明器而不是Isabelle，由于不同的策略和自动化，这极大地影响了性能，并且与我们的方法不直接可比。

items.en: When informal proofs are generated, we condition a large language model on informal statements to sample 100100100 informal proofs per problem. Specifically, we use the Codex code-davinci-002 model (Chen et al., 2021) through the OpenAI API, and the 888B, 626262B, and 540540540B versions of the Minerva model from Lewkowycz et al. (2022). We use greedy decoding for Codex, and nucleus sampling (Holtzman et al., 2019) with temperature T=0.6𝑇0.6T=0.6 and top​_​p=0.95top_p0.95\textrm{top}\_\textrm{p}=0.95 for Minerva models.
items.zh: 当生成非形式化证明时，我们以非形式化陈述为条件，对每个问题采样100100100个非形式化证明。具体来说，我们通过OpenAI API使用Codex code-davinci-002模型（Chen等人，2021），以及Lewkowycz等人（2022）的Minerva模型的888B、626262B和540540540B版本。我们对Codex使用贪心解码，对Minerva模型使用核采样（Holtzman等人，2019），温度T=0.6，top_p=0.95。

items.en: For sketching, we manually prepare 202020 autoformalization examples of the format (((informal statement, informal proof, formal statement, formal sketch))), to form a pool of high-quality demonstrations. Of these 202020 examples, 101010 are of the algebra type and 101010 are of the number theory type. All examples are from the validation set of the miniF2F dataset and can be found in the supplementary materials. The sketches contain in-line comments as in Figure 2. If the name of the problem gives away its type (algebra or number theory), we only use examples of the corresponding type. We also ensure that the sampled few-shot examples do not contain the problem being solved. The prompt is composed of 333 uniformly randomly sampled example from the pool and the current problem’s (informal statement, informal proof, formal statement). We use this prompt to query the same Codex model to get the desired proof sketches. We use greedy decoding and a maximum of 204820482048 tokens in the generated sequence. For all the experiments, unless stated otherwise, we control the total number of queries made to Codex per problem to be 100100100. This means 100100100 queries per human informal solution and one query per language-model-generated solution.
items.zh: 对于草拟，我们手动准备了202020个格式为（非形式化陈述，非形式化证明，形式化陈述，形式化草图）的自动形式化示例，以形成一个高质量示范池。在这202020个示例中，101010个是代数类型，101010个是数论类型。所有示例均来自miniF2F数据集的验证集，可在补充材料中找到。草图包含如图2所示的行内注释。如果问题的名称揭示了其类型（代数或数论），我们只使用相应类型的示例。我们还确保采样的少样本示例不包含正在解决的问题。提示由从池中均匀随机采样的333个示例和当前问题的（非形式化陈述，非形式化证明，形式化陈述）组成。我们使用此提示查询相同的Codex模型以获得所需的证明草图。我们使用贪心解码，生成序列中最多20482048个token。对于所有实验，除非另有说明，我们控制每个问题对Codex的总查询次数为100100次。这意味着每个人类非形式化解对应100次查询，每个语言模型生成的解对应一次查询。

items.en: To prove the conjectures left open by the formal sketch, we use the Sledgehammer + heuristics automated prover described in Subsection 4.2. We execute the automated prover on every open conjecture in the sketch to synthesize a formal proof that can be verified by Isabelle.
items.zh: 为了证明形式化草图留下的猜想，我们使用第4.2小节中描述的Sledgehammer + 启发式自动证明器。我们对草图中的每个开放猜想执行自动证明器，以合成一个可由Isabelle验证的形式化证明。

items.en: In Table 1, we display the proportion of successful formal proofs found on the miniF2F dataset . The results include the four baselines described in Subsection 4.2 and the DSP method with human-written proofs and model-generated proofs. From the table, we can see that the automated prover with 111111 additional heuristic tactics significantly increases the performance of Sledgehammer, boosting its success rate from 9.9%percent9.99.9\% to 18.0%percent18.018.0\% on the validation set of miniF2F and from 10.4%percent10.410.4\% to 20.9%percent20.920.9\% on the test set. The two baselines using language models and proof search (Thor and Thor + expert iteration) achieve success rates of 29.9%percent29.929.9\% and 35.2%percent35.235.2\% on the test set of miniF2F, respectively.
items.zh: 在表1中，我们展示了在miniF2F数据集上成功找到的形式化证明的比例。结果包括第4.2小节中描述的四个基线和DSP方法（使用人类编写的证明和模型生成的证明）。从表中可以看出，具有111111个额外启发式策略的自动证明器显著提高了Sledgehammer的性能，在miniF2F验证集上将其成功率从9.9%提升到18.0%，在测试集上从10.4%提升到20.9%。使用语言模型和证明搜索的两个基线（Thor和Thor + 专家迭代）在miniF2F测试集上分别达到了29.9%和35.2%的成功率。

items.en: With informal proofs written by humans, the DSP method achieves success rates of 42.6%percent42.642.6\% and 39.3%percent39.339.3\% on the validation and test sets of miniF2F. A total of 200200200 out of 488488488 problems can be proved in this way. The Codex model and the Minerva (888B) model give very similar results in solving problems on miniF2F: they both guide the automated prover to solve 40.6%percent40.640.6\% and 35.3%percent35.335.3\% of problems on the validation and the test sets respectively. This is corroborated by Lewkowycz et al. (2022)’s observation that these two models have comparable performance in solving mathematical problems.
items.zh: 使用人类编写的非形式化证明，DSP方法在miniF2F验证集和测试集上分别达到了42.6%和39.3%的成功率。总共488个问题中有200200个可以这样证明。Codex模型和Minerva（888B）模型在解决miniF2F问题上给出了非常相似的结果：它们分别引导自动证明器在验证集和测试集上解决了40.6%和35.3%的问题。这与Lewkowycz等人（2022）的观察一致，即这两个模型在解决数学问题方面具有可比性能。

items.en: When we switch to the Minerva (626262B) model, the success rates rise up to 43.9%percent43.943.9\% and 37.7%percent37.737.7\% respectively. Compared to human-written informal proofs, its success rates are 1.3%percent1.31.3\% higher on the validation set and 1.6%percent1.61.6\% lower on the test set. In total, the Minerva (626262B) model is able to solve 199199199 problems on miniF2F, one fewer than with human proofs. The Minerva (540540540B) model solves 42.6%percent42.642.6\% and 38.9%percent38.938.9\% of problems in the validation and the test sets of miniF2F, also resulting in 199199199 successful proofs. The DSP method is effective in guiding the automated prover under both settings: using human informal proofs or language-model-generated informal proofs. DSP almost doubles the prover’s success rate and results in a new state-of-the-art performance on miniF2F with Isabelle. Moreover, the larger Minerva models are almost as helpful as a human in guiding the automated formal prover.
items.zh: 当我们切换到Minerva（626262B）模型时，成功率分别上升到43.9%和37.7%。与人类编写的非形式化证明相比，其在验证集上高出1.3%，在测试集上低1.6%。总体而言，Minerva（626262B）模型能够解决miniF2F上的199199个问题，比人类证明少一个。Minerva（540540540B）模型在miniF2F验证集和测试集上分别解决了42.6%和38.9%的问题，也产生了199199个成功证明。DSP方法在两种设置下都能有效引导自动证明器：使用人类非形式化证明或语言模型生成的非形式化证明。DSP几乎使证明器的成功率翻倍，并在使用Isabelle的miniF2F上实现了新的最先进性能。此外，更大的Minerva模型在引导自动形式化证明器方面几乎与人类一样有帮助。

items.en: To facilitate the alignment between the informal proofs and the formal proof sketches, we copy relevant segments of the informal proofs as in-line comments in the sketches. In the manually constructed prompt examples, these comments are prefixed to the corresponding Isabelle code blocks, as shown in Figure 2 (the text in red). We hypothesize that this technique is beneficial for large language models to synthesize formal sketches. To validate this hypothesis, we perform an ablation study by removing the in-line comments in the prompt examples before running the experiment. The results are displayed in Table 1. We find that without in-line comments, the success rates drop by 4.9%percent4.94.9\% and 2.8%percent2.82.8\% on the validation and test sets respectively. We conclude that having in-line comments is helpful for generating formal proof sketches.
items.zh: 为了促进非形式化证明与形式化证明草图之间的对齐，我们将非形式化证明的相关片段复制为草图中的行内注释。在手动构建的提示示例中，这些注释被前缀到相应的Isabelle代码块之前，如图2所示（红色文本）。我们假设这种技术有利于大型语言模型合成形式化草图。为了验证这一假设，我们进行了一项消融研究，在运行实验之前移除提示示例中的行内注释。结果如表1所示。我们发现，没有行内注释，验证集和测试集上的成功率分别下降了4.9%和2.8%。我们得出结论，行内注释有助于生成形式化证明草图。

items.en: Drafting informal proofs is the first step of the DSP method. To investigate the necessity of this step, we perform an experiment of formal sketching and proving without informal proofs at all. Because formal proof sketches are written in the declarative proof style, they are fairly similar to the informal proof drafts already. Concretely, we remove the informal proofs and the in-line comments (because they are copied segments of the informal proofs) in the prompt examples. This removes the need for the informal proof writer, whether a human or a neural network. The results of this setup are shown in Table 1. It can be seen that the success rates on the validation and the test sets of miniF2F drop by 3.7%percent3.73.7\% and 5.3%percent5.35.3\% respectively compared to with human-written proofs. They are also inferior to success rates obtained with language-model-generated informal proofs. This demonstrates the importance of drafting informal proofs before sketching and proving.
items.zh: 起草非形式化证明是DSP方法的第一步。为了研究这一步骤的必要性，我们进行了一项实验，完全在没有非形式化证明的情况下进行形式化草拟和证明。由于形式化证明草图是以声明式证明风格编写的，它们已经与非形式化证明草稿非常相似。具体来说，我们移除了提示示例中的非形式化证明和行内注释（因为它们是非形式化证明的复制片段）。这消除了对非形式化证明编写者（无论是人类还是神经网络）的需求。此设置的结果如表1所示。可以看出，与使用人类编写的证明相比，miniF2F验证集和测试集上的成功率分别下降了3.7%和5.3%。它们也低于使用语言模型生成的非形式化证明所获得的成功率。这证明了在草拟和证明之前起草非形式化证明的重要性。

items.en: Using an autoformalizer to generate proof sketches which are then completed by an automated prover is central to our method. The effect of utilizing an automated prover to close open conjectures in proof sketches is worth studying, so we conduct an ablation experiment for it. Namely, we replace the proof sketches in the prompt examples with complete formal proofs. The complete formal proofs still follow the declarative proof style, but do not contain any open conjectures. As a result, the large language model will also generate full proofs instead of sketches, and we directly check whether these generated proofs are valid. The results in this setup are presented in Table 1. The results reveal that without an automated prover to close open conjectures, the success rate on miniF2F decreases by 9.8%percent9.89.8\% and 9.0%percent9.09.0\% on the validation and test sets respectively. The drastic performance difference indicates the essential role of automated provers in our approach.
items.zh: 使用自动形式化器生成证明草图，然后由自动证明器完成，是我们方法的核心。利用自动证明器来关闭证明草图中的开放猜想的效果值得研究，因此我们为此进行了一项消融实验。即，我们将提示示例中的证明草图替换为完整的形式化证明。完整的形式化证明仍然遵循声明式证明风格，但不包含任何开放猜想。结果，大型语言模型也将生成完整的证明而不是草图，我们直接检查这些生成的证明是否有效。此设置的结果如表1所示。结果表明，如果没有自动证明器来关闭开放猜想，miniF2F验证集和测试集上的成功率分别下降了9.8%和9.0%。性能的巨大差异表明自动证明器在我们方法中的关键作用。

items.en: To understand the effect of the ablations on the DSP method’s scaling properties, we vary the number of autoformalization attempts per problem and plot the number of successful proofs found on the miniF2F dataset in Figure 3 (left). Four methods are contrasted: the original DSP method with human informal proofs, the DSP method without in-line comments, the DSP method without informal proofs, and the DSP method without formal proof sketches. It can be seen from the figure that with the original DSP method, the performance reaches a plateau (no new proofs are found) after 707070 autoformalization attempts are made for each problem. For the ablation study with no in-line comments, the plateau is reached much faster, after around 505050 autoformalization attempts. This method solves 181181181 problems in total. The ablation study without informal proofs also reaches a plateau at around 707070 autoformalization attempts, solving 178178178 problems in total. The ablation study without sketching can solve 154154154 problems on miniF2F. In comparison, with human informal proofs, only 777 autoformalization attempts are required to reach this performance.
items.zh: 为了理解消融对DSP方法缩放属性的影响，我们改变每个问题的自动形式化尝试次数，并在图3（左）中绘制在miniF2F数据集上找到的成功证明数量。对比了四种方法：原始DSP方法（使用人类非形式化证明）、无行内注释的DSP方法、无非形式化证明的DSP方法、无形式化证明草图的DSP方法。从图中可以看出，使用原始DSP方法，在对每个问题进行7070次自动形式化尝试后，性能达到平台期（没有找到新的证明）。对于无行内注释的消融研究，平台期达到得更快，大约在5050次自动形式化尝试后。该方法总共解决了181181个问题。无非形式化证明的消融研究也在大约7070次自动形式化尝试后达到平台期，总共解决了178178个问题。无草图的消融研究可以在miniF2F上解决154154个问题。相比之下，使用人类非形式化证明，只需777次自动形式化尝试即可达到此性能。

items.en: Our experiments demonstrated that model-generated informal proofs from Minerva and Codex can help guide a formal theorem prover. In this section, we analyze the properties of these proofs further. We focus on the informal proofs the 626262B and 540540540B Minerva models produce in this section, as they give the best overall performances and achieve the highest success rate on miniF2F.
items.zh: 我们的实验表明，来自Minerva和Codex的模型生成的非形式化证明可以帮助引导形式化定理证明器。在本节中，我们进一步分析这些证明的属性。我们重点分析626262B和540540540B Minerva模型产生的非形式化证明，因为它们给出了最佳的整体性能，并在miniF2F上实现了最高成功率。

items.en: Interestingly, our approach manages to solve one problem from the International Mathematical Olympiad (imo_1959_1) with a Minerva-generated solution, but not with the human proof. For this problem, we present the successful Minerva-generated informal proof draft and the formal proof in Figure 4. We hypothesize that the reason behind this phenomenon is that human proofs might leave gaps between conjectures that are too difficult for automated provers to solve. On the other hand, the diversity in language model informal proofs makes some of them more amenable to automated provers. In Appendix C, we analyze the human and the Minerva informal proofs for this problem in greater detail.
items.zh: 有趣的是，我们的方法成功使用Minerva生成的解解决了一个国际数学奥林匹克问题（imo_1959_1），但使用人类证明却没有。对于这个问题，我们在图4中展示了成功的Minerva生成的非形式化证明草稿和形式化证明。我们假设这种现象背后的原因是人类证明可能在猜想之间留下了对自动证明器来说过于困难的间隙。另一方面，语言模型非形式化证明的多样性使得其中一些更易于自动证明器处理。在附录C中，我们更详细地分析了该问题的人类和Minerva非形式化证明。

items.en: Next, we analyze the relationship between the validity of the formal proofs and the correctness of the informal proofs. For our analysis, we randomly sample 505050 Minerva proofs of different problems, which are then successfully converted to formal proofs. We then manually evaluate the correctness of these 505050 informal proofs. Among them, 292929 proofs (58%percent5858\%) are entirely correct, 161616 are incorrect with a clearly identifiable incorrect step, and 555 “proofs” are nonsensical and simply rephrase the final conclusions of the problems.
items.zh: 接下来，我们分析形式化证明的有效性与非形式化证明的正确性之间的关系。为了分析，我们随机采样了5050个不同问题的Minerva证明，这些证明随后被成功转换为形式化证明。然后我们手动评估这5050个非形式化证明的正确性。其中，2929个证明（58%）完全正确，1616个不正确且存在明显可识别的错误步骤，还有5个“证明”毫无意义，只是重述了问题的最终结论。

items.en: Seeing that a total of 16+5=211652116+5=21 incorrect informal proofs can lead to successful formal proofs, we study how they guide the automated formal prover despite having flaws themselves. The 212121 proofs divide into 222 cases: In the first case, we find 131313 problems for which the informal proofs are mostly ignored, and the automated prover can find proofs by itself; In the other 888 problems, although the informal proofs are wrong, the autoformalizer manages to correct them, either by ignoring the erroneous steps or by stating their correct versions in the formal proof sketches. This suggests that the autoformalizer has some understanding of the mathematical statements and is not merely translating them from an informal language to a formal language. It is robust to slight noises in its input. In Appendix D, we present 444 case studies comparing the human and Minerva informal proofs. Particularly, LABEL:fig:minerva_cases shows a completely correct example and one example of each pathological case.
items.zh: 看到总共16+5=21个不正确的非形式化证明可以导致成功的形式化证明，我们研究它们尽管存在缺陷，但如何引导自动形式化证明器。这21个证明分为两种情况：第一种情况，我们发现13个问题，其中非形式化证明基本上被忽略，自动证明器可以自行找到证明；在另外8个问题中，尽管非形式化证明是错误的，但自动形式化器设法纠正了它们，要么忽略错误步骤，要么在形式化证明草图中陈述其正确版本。这表明自动形式化器对数学陈述有一定的理解，而不仅仅是将其从非形式化语言翻译成形式化语言。它对输入中的轻微噪声具有鲁棒性。在附录D中，我们展示了4个案例研究，比较了人类和Minerva的非形式化证明。特别是，图LABEL:fig:minerva_cases展示了一个完全正确的示例和每个病理情况的一个示例。

items.en: Is there a way to detect which Minerva proofs are correct, without human evaluation? For a preliminary investigation, we filter out all the problems that can be solved directly with the automated prover from the 505050 and are left with 272727 informal proofs. Of these 272727, 212121 are completely correct, 666 still contain small errors, but none are nonsensical. With this simple filter, we achieve a precision of 77.8%percent77.877.8\% and a recall of 72.4%percent72.472.4\% in identifying correct Minerva informal proofs.
items.zh: 有没有一种方法可以在没有人类评估的情况下检测哪些Minerva证明是正确的？为了初步研究，我们从50个中过滤掉所有可以直接用自动证明器解决的问题，剩下27个非形式化证明。在这27个中，21个完全正确，6个仍然包含小错误，但没有一个是毫无意义的。通过这个简单的过滤器，我们在识别正确的Minerva非形式化证明方面达到了77.8%的精确率和72.4%的召回率。

items.en: To understand the influence of different informal proof sources on the scaling properties of DSP, we plot the number of successful proofs found on miniF2F against the number of autoformalization attempts per problem in Figure 3 (right). Note that for each problem, we have 111 informal proof by a human and 100100100 informal proof drafts by each language model. The one human proof is used 100100100 times for formal proof sketch generation, while each language model proof draft is used only once. We notice that the 626262B Minerva model and the 540540540B Minerva model always have comparable performances. Considering that the 540540540B Minerva model is more capable of mathematical reasoning (Lewkowycz et al., 2022, Table 3) than the 626262B model, we hypothesize that the bottleneck in the DSP process shifts from drafting to sketching and proving. I.e., informal proof drafts of higher quality do not necessarily lead to more successful formal proofs due to the limitation of sketching and proving. Both the 626262B and the 540540540B models result in more successful proofs than the smaller (888B) Minerva model and the Codex model, consistently for any number of attempts. The 888B Minerva model and the Codex model behave similarly, both finding 185185185 proofs in the end. Informal proofs written by humans help solve more problems than those by Minerva models for 1−10011001-100 autoformalization attempts. However, the difference is small (111 problem) when 100100100 are made.
items.zh: 为了理解不同非正式证明来源对DSP缩放性质的影响，我们在图3（右）中绘制了miniF2F上成功证明的数量与每个问题自动形式化尝试次数的关系。注意，对于每个问题，我们有一个人类撰写的非正式证明和每个语言模型生成的100个非正式证明草稿。人类证明被用于100次形式化证明草稿生成，而每个语言模型证明草稿仅使用一次。我们注意到，62B Minerva模型和540B Minerva模型的性能始终相当。考虑到540B Minerva模型比62B模型更擅长数学推理（Lewkowycz等人，2022，表3），我们假设DSP过程中的瓶颈从草稿阶段转移到了草稿和证明阶段。即，由于草稿和证明阶段的限制，更高质量的非正式证明草稿不一定能带来更多成功的形式化证明。无论是62B还是540B模型，在任意尝试次数下，都比较小的（8B）Minerva模型和Codex模型产生了更多成功证明。8B Minerva模型和Codex模型表现相似，最终都找到了185个证明。在1到100次自动形式化尝试中，人类撰写的非正式证明比Minerva模型帮助解决了更多问题。然而，当尝试次数达到100时，差异很小（1个问题）。

items.en: Noticing that the number of successful proofs does not plateau for the Minerva-generated proofs, we investigate how further increasing the number of autoformalization attempts changes the number of problems solved for human-written and language-model-generated proofs. For each problem, we use 111 human informal proof and sample 200200200 sketches for it; we use the same 100100100 informal proof drafts by the Minerva (540540540B) language model and sample 222 sketches for each draft. The total number of sketches per problem is 200200200 in both settings. We plot the number of proofs solved with respect to the number of sketches in Figure 5 (left). We find that with human informal proofs, 203203203 theorems (106/9710697106/97 on valid/test) have successful formal proofs after 200200200 attempts. While with language-model-generated informal proofs, 209209209 theorems (111/9811198111/98 on valid/test) have successful formal proofs after the same number of autoformalization attempts. This suggests that with enough autoformalization attempts, the diversity in language-model-generated informal proofs can benefit the automated formalization process more than the “ground-truth” human informal proofs.
items.zh: 注意到Minerva生成的证明的成功数量并未趋于平稳，我们研究了进一步增加自动形式化尝试次数如何改变人类撰写和语言模型生成证明所解决的问题数量。对于每个问题，我们使用1个人类非正式证明并为其采样200个草稿；我们使用Minerva（540B）语言模型生成的相同100个非正式证明草稿，并为每个草稿采样2个草稿。两种设置下每个问题的草稿总数均为200。我们在图5（左）中绘制了成功证明数量与草稿数量的关系。我们发现，使用人类非正式证明时，经过200次尝试后，有203个定理（验证集/测试集上106/97）获得了成功的形式化证明。而使用语言模型生成的非正式证明时，经过相同次数的自动形式化尝试后，有209个定理（验证集/测试集上111/98）获得了成功的形式化证明。这表明，在足够的自动形式化尝试次数下，语言模型生成的非正式证明的多样性比“真实”的人类非正式证明更能使自动形式化过程受益。

items.en: In Section 4, language models generate 100100100 informal proof drafts for each mathematical problem and the autoformalizer is used once on each draft. It is likely that some drafts have the potential to be formalized correctly, but do not get to produce a successful sketch because the randomly sampled examples in the prompt are not suitable. We would like to reduce this variance by attempting autoformalization multiple times, but it is expensive to do so. Therefore we conduct an experiment to investigate what the optimal way of allocating drafts and sketches per draft. For the Minerva (540540540 B) model, we vary the number of informal proof drafts and the number of formal proof sketches per draft, under the constraint that the total number of sketches per problem is fewer than 100100100. We present the number of miniF2F problems solved under every combination in Figure 5 (right). The plot shows that when the total number of autoformalization attempts is fixed, increasing the number of drafts per problem yields the most successes on miniF2F.
items.zh: 在第4节中，语言模型为每个数学问题生成100个非正式证明草稿，并且自动形式化器对每个草稿使用一次。很可能有些草稿有潜力被正确形式化，但由于提示中随机采样的示例不合适而未能生成成功的草稿。我们希望通过多次尝试自动形式化来减少这种方差，但这样做成本高昂。因此，我们进行了一项实验，研究如何最优地分配草稿数量和每个草稿的草稿数量。对于Minerva（540B）模型，我们在每个问题的草稿总数少于100的约束下，改变非正式证明草稿的数量和每个草稿的形式化证明草稿数量。我们在图5（右）中展示了每种组合下解决的miniF2F问题数量。该图显示，当自动形式化尝试总数固定时，增加每个问题的草稿数量在miniF2F上取得了最多的成功。

items.en: This work utilizes two language models that have been trained on a large amount of internet data. Several prior works (Trinh & Le, 2018; Carlini et al., 2022) pointed out that such models can memorize some fraction of the data they encounter during training. For drafting informal proofs, we mainly experimented with Minerva. Lewkowycz et al. (2022, Section 5) discussed the memorization effects within Minerva and concluded that they could not find evidence that its abilities are due to memorization. For the autoformalization of proof sketches, the Codex (code-davinci-002) model was used. Its training data was collected before June 2021333https://beta.openai.com/docs/models/codex-series-private-beta, at which time the miniF2F dataset had not been made public. So the model cannot benefit from memorizing the exact problems and proofs. Therefore, it is inappropriate to attribute the abilities of models used in this paper to memorization.
items.zh: 本工作使用了两个在大量互联网数据上训练的语言模型。之前的几项工作（Trinh & Le, 2018; Carlini等人，2022）指出，这类模型可能会记住训练中遇到的部分数据。在草拟非正式证明时，我们主要使用Minerva进行实验。Lewkowycz等人（2022，第5节）讨论了Minerva中的记忆效应，并得出结论，他们无法找到证据表明其能力源于记忆。对于证明草稿的自动形式化，我们使用了Codex（code-davinci-002）模型。其训练数据收集于2021年6月之前，当时miniF2F数据集尚未公开。因此，该模型无法通过记忆确切的问题和证明而受益。因此，将本文所用模型的能力归因于记忆是不恰当的。

items.en: In this paper, we introduced Draft, Sketch, and Prove (DSP), a novel approach that takes advantage of informal proofs to synthesize formal proofs. We demonstrated its feasibility and effectiveness by reaching state-of-the-art performance on the miniF2F dataset with the Isabelle theorem prover. Central to our method are formal proof sketches that mirror the high-level reasoning structures of informal proofs. Our ablations showed that the ability to automatically convert informal proofs to proof sketches is critical to the success of DSP.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了草稿、草稿与证明（DSP），这是一种利用非正式证明来合成形式化证明的新方法。我们通过在Isabelle定理证明器上达到miniF2F数据集的最先进性能，证明了其可行性和有效性。我们方法的核心是形式化证明草稿，它反映了非正式证明的高层推理结构。我们的消融实验表明，自动将非正式证明转换为证明草稿的能力对DSP的成功至关重要。

items.en: Our DSP method differs fundamentally from previous applications of machine learning to formal proof synthesis in two aspects. Firstly, while most approaches in the field focus on improving proof search, our method seeks to construct the entire formal proof structure from the informal proof in one decoding operation. The task of the automated prover is then simplified to filling the gaps between intermediate conjectures. Secondly, while existing approaches operate exclusively on formal data, DSP by design benefits from informal proofs.
items.zh: 我们的DSP方法在两个方面与先前机器学习在形式化证明合成中的应用有根本不同。首先，虽然该领域的大多数方法侧重于改进证明搜索，我们的方法旨在通过一次解码操作从非正式证明构建整个形式化证明结构。自动证明器的任务因此简化为填补中间猜想之间的空白。其次，虽然现有方法仅操作形式化数据，DSP通过设计受益于非正式证明。

items.en: In this work, we utilized a purely symbolic automated prover to close the gaps in proof sketches. In the future, we aim to equip DSP with more powerful mechanisms, such as HyperTree Proof Search (Lample et al., 2022), to broaden the scope of provable theorems. Similar to AlphaCode (Li et al., 2022), we found that the number of generations is crucial for performance. The computational cost of the autoformalizer being a bottleneck in our method, we seek to develop approaches able to generate high-quality proof sketches more efficiently.
items.zh: 在本工作中，我们使用纯符号自动证明器来填补证明草稿中的空白。未来，我们旨在为DSP配备更强大的机制，例如超树证明搜索（Lample等人，2022），以拓宽可证明定理的范围。与AlphaCode（Li等人，2022）类似，我们发现生成次数对性能至关重要。自动形式化器的计算成本是我们方法中的瓶颈，我们寻求开发能够更高效生成高质量证明草稿的方法。

items.en: The code for experiment reproduction is at github.com/albertqjiang/draft_sketch_prove. Additional result details for main experiments can be found in the same repository.
items.zh: 实验复现代码位于github.com/albertqjiang/draft_sketch_prove。主要实验的附加结果详情可在同一仓库中找到。

items.en: AQJ conceived the idea of using proof sketches and conducted the experiments. SW constructed the first version of the pipeline and the initial autoformalization prompts. JPZ produced the Minerva informal proofs, and helped conduct autoformalization experiments. GL proposed to use inline comments in formal proof sketches to improve alignment. JPZ, YW, and SW performed the case analyses of Minerva solutions. AQJ, TL, GL, SW, and JL contributed to the dataset. AQJ and WL wrote the final autoformalization prompts. MJ is AQJ’s PhD supervisor. AQJ, GL, SW, and TL wrote the paper. YW and GL directed the project.
items.zh: AQJ构思了使用证明草稿的想法并进行了实验。SW构建了管道的第一个版本和初始自动形式化提示。JPZ生成了Minerva非正式证明，并帮助进行了自动形式化实验。GL提出在形式化证明草稿中使用内联注释以改进对齐。JPZ、YW和SW对Minerva解法进行了案例分析。AQJ、TL、GL、SW和JL对数据集做出了贡献。AQJ和WL撰写了最终的自动形式化提示。MJ是AQJ的博士导师。AQJ、GL、SW和TL撰写了论文。YW和GL指导了该项目。
